name: The Rustlers V2
text: A nameless man attempts to find a spring of oil in the forest of oil and spines to keep his lantern burning, while the darkness all around him murmurs with the voices of demons (rustlers) who can only materialize in the dark.

name: Act 1
text: A nameless man attempts to find a spring of oil in the forest of oil and spines to keep his lantern burning, while the darkness all around him murmurs with the voices of demons (rustlers) who can only materialize in the dark.

name: Chapter 1
text: A nameless man attempts to find a spring of oil in the forest of oil and spines to keep his lantern burning, while the darkness all around him murmurs with the voices of demons (rustlers) who can only materialize in the dark.

name: Scene 1
text: Introduce the nameless man, the environment, the man's equipment, The Rustlers, and the threat posed by the forest. Introduce his goal.

name: Scene 2
text: Introduce the wisp, the threat of the rustlers, the Underboughers, the man's routines/habits, his expectations. Hint at a lack of preparation on his part. Describe his reliance on focus and determination.

name: Scene 3
text: The man arrives at a pace of rest called The Mouth, but must leave. He searches for and finds an oil spring. The man presses on, though the wisp wishes to stay.

name: Scene 4
text: The first accident: the man crashes through a hole in the roots. He just manages to get it fixed, then tries to press on.

name: Scene 5
text: The second accident, he trips and breaks his lamp. He finds another pool, but can't get the lamp repaired this time.

name: Scene 6
text: The man contemplates capturing the wisp. He gives up, then runs. The Rustlers haunt him.

name: Scene 7
text: The man accepts his fate.

name: Wormapple Woods
text: Two dog crusaders, Cemetery Stones and Greymalkin, are sent to hunt down a deserter, but take a wrong turn along the way.

name: Adoration of the Foe
text: The relationship between Greymalkin and Stones, the Wormapple wood of albuma trees, The Restless Wastes, and the dog crusade. Foreshadow The Doomed. Introduce the rats that live in the trunks (the enemy), as well as the tracks of a cloven beast (a wild hog which the rats grow out of; the master foe). The two are forced to take shelter in a cave because of a radioactive rainstorm.

name: Adoration Chapter 1
text: The relationship between Greymalkin and Stones, the Wormapple wood of skeletal albuma trees, The Restless Wastes, and the dog crusade. Foreshadow The Doomed. Introduce the rats that live in the trunks (the enemy), as well as the tracks of a cloven beast (a wild hog which the rats grow out of; the master foe). The two are forced to take shelter in a cave because of a radioactive rainstorm.

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text: "Greymalkin is jumping at noises, and asks Stones if there are Doomed nearby. Stones tracks The Ghoul to a crossroads, and they pick a direction." Stones tell Greymalkin to wear his kit if he's concerned. Greymalkin asks if their gear can kill doomed. Stones explains that Doomed Rats don't infect humans - fortunately, otherwise they'd all be dead; Greymalkin asks what they do infect; Stones explains Dogs mostly, cats, pigs Greymalkin fumbles with snares and clawtraps; stones tells him it probably won't work with doomed rats; greymalkin asks what if it's bigger than rats, stones says they rarely find dogs or other mammals in this part of the waste, but greymalkin presses hint at the ratboar in scene 1 with rats Stones reassures Greymalkin with his knife They arrive at a crossroads; one way leading to the gravelands; one way leading to the woods Stones says: "We'll find the ghoul. Or we'll find an empty wood. Or we'll find something else." (save description/texture for next part)

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
"Greymalkin and Stones arrive at Wormapple Wood; they skirmish with the rats."
Describe a SPECIFIC LOCATION in the woods, a small and broken shrine at the side of a riverbank; stumps like swiss cheese; the silver, opaque water; the red clouds above; the old man's beard swaying from the skeletons of the trees which remain; the shelf mushrooms filled with dripping water; the scurrying; an old fishing boat at the side of the water
Stones asks what the shrine is, Greymalkin explains it is to a specific constellation/philosophy; Greymalkin reads the Psalm written on the shrine (Mork Borg PG 17)
Soon the forest closed in around the pair, dense and skeletal; Tree branches rattling in a lonely wind
Greymalkin stares at himself in the mirror-sheen of the river (way for narrator to introduce his own looks; greymalkin has charcoal eyes with black irises)
Foreshadow the village of Hovart's Field; one of the two mentions they heard the village is cursed
Greymalkin wonders why the river doesn't move
Stones suggests that the ghoul would follow the river to a town; stones suggests they follow it
The boat looks sturdy

name: 4. False Hopes
text: "Stones brags about how they've skirted any danger this way and can search more quickly along both banks, but Greymalkin points out movement in the stumps on the hills." Stones tries to put greymalkin at ease by joking which of them is going to kill the ghoul (of course it's stones) They come to a narrow gorge in the river, with the dry stumps of dessicated trees cemented into the stone and earth walls on either side Rats start dropping down (greymalkin hastily puts on his gear) Stones kill them or throws them out wearing his leather gloves; says rats aren't the worst of the doomed They see the broken, splintered, sharp poles of a wood bridge ahead, but can probably pass through Suddenly a branch snaps above and smashes through their boat Greymalkin (who can't swim) is gapsing and floudering, feels rats swimming around him Stones grabs him and pulls him along to a small bank at the base of the cliffs both are soaking wet and shivering under a grey sky

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text: "Greymalkin and Stones are forced to walk along the embankment beneath the cliff, and seek shelter from the cold." Greymalkin shivering, observes their bleak surroundings, then suddenly worries about his journals. Stones grumbles about losing his hat (RUNNING JOKE IN THE STORIES, he is bald); jokes that they'll freeze to death, but at least they won't succumb to the doomed. Greymalkin says they need fire, Stones says they need to get back to the woods; they check their gear and move out. Describe more of their gear (molotovs, leather armor, cloth masks, etc) They climb up a sandy bank, back into the woods; greymalking jumping at sounds, which stones says are rats They hear heavy scurrying from the stumps, they ready their gear; “Present thyselves!”

name: 6. The Master Appears
text: As the pair enter the Wormapple Woods, the doomed rats emerge from their burrows with the onset of night. Stones is picking up sticks and handing them to Greymalkin, trying to be grimly cheerful. As one of the swarms charges them, stones manages to get one of the burnbottles lit. He throws it, temporarily warding off the rats. They have backed into a cliff; by the fire created from the burnbottle, they see a cave up on the face of the cliff. Stones and Greymalkin fling and kick rats aside, wearing their gear, clambering up the rock slope to the cave. They climb inside. Just then, they hear a squeal from behind (a sound like a swine meeting a tree limb splitting from the trunk. End the scene on a strange hushed note, bleached of color…

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text: Transition ahead in time. Night sets, with a bitter cold that silences and stills even the rats, as Stones and Greymalkin set a fire and warm themselves in the cave. Huge textural description of the cavern; an enormous chamber of half-worked stone, an ancient shed with stacked stones walls and a broken shingle roof and a firepit; the smell of oil thick about the place; huge tiles, and a huge angle pipe broken and leading into the floor (a former pump station for the river slough); scarlet moss on the ledges; Greymalkin has frost on his mustache. The hut was the hut of a chanter-hermit among the Golani, long abandoned, who must have taken up residence at the pump station. Greymalkin shivers, and observes frost around a pool of water; stones builds a fire and threatens to kill greymalkin (jovially) if he doesn't help - forces greymalkin to give paper from his journal; Greymalkin gives blank pages. Greymalkin keeps glancing at the entry, and hears the rats, but they don't come in. End with Stones and Greymalkin feeling trapped in the wormapple woods in the cave. Stones strikes, strikes, strikes at the piled kindling with firesteel. Just as greymalkin's teeth begin to chatter, the fire blooms yellow on the cave walls

name: The Heroics
text:
Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

Introduce Themes (especially necessary evil)
Show how Stones is cruel, but also active, and willing to do what is horrible (he is the only one willing to kill people, while others aren't)

name: Heroics: Chapter 1
text: (Usually 1 Chapter) Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: 8. Introduction
text:
Greymalkin takes out and inspects a map, but can find no bearing.

Stones goes to the entry and listens, the woods are cold and silent; Greymalkin looks at the old pump mechanisms, and recites  a few golani prayers  he finds inscribed on the stone

Stones starts to bully greymalkin, forces him to give him a drink, torments greymalkin by making him think that Stones is about to abuse him; then stones laughs, lets him go, and says he's not a sodomite (though he warns greymalkin there are some who are like that in the crusaders)

Minor description of an owl hooing in the night; Stones jokes that there are still some natural creatures out there

Stones gathers some rocks, says Birdsey back at camp like to throw them at birds (good practice), but he's never hit an own before - Stones wants to bring it back and show him

Eerie, hypnotic, ritualistic scene; lull the audience into uncanny world of no precedent

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
Greymalkin observes how inhospitable the waste seems, marveling that any civilization could have once flourished in such a place; Stones muses about a story he heard; that The Restless Waste was fine until the death of the sun god Theman, at which point all the Golani machines went crazy, becoming restless

Stones starts eating some bugs; finds a hewer among the tools; he bangs it around a bit to break the weapon, then suggests they show it to the captain as proof that the ghoul is dead

Greymalkin kicks a rat away from the entrance, and shivers; he finds a puzzle sphere, but it could go to any contraption in the waste; he stuffs it in a bag

Greymalkin says he thought Stones wanted to kill; Stones says he'll do it if there something killworthy nearby, but he's not going to chase after kills in the cold; he reasons to Greymalkin that the captain probably just wanted Greymalkin to get some experience in the waste

Stones asks greymalkin how he got assigned out to the waste; greymalkin refuses to answer; stones laughs like a sadist and says suit yourself; then he tells greymalkin his earliest memory, a rotting plague-ship rolling vacant across the girting sea, thudding into the grey sand of Leeges

One minor description of the fire, sinking slowly

Just as they catch the sun rising red in the misty stump wood, Greymalkin hears a sound from the broken, slanted pipe

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
Describe The Squealer / Ratboar emerging slowly from the pipe-tunnel; it is covered in swiss-cheese holes with rats crawling through it; it has no tusks, but instead a mouth full of biting rat heads - for this whole description, gradually increase the speed of the sentences; if you can, structure the description with 11 distinct beats or keywords.

The boar and the rats pouring from its body attack; Stones hacks and prods at the boar with the broken hewer, while greymalkin smushes rats.

Continue to describe the vicious, bloody combat; the rats continue to writhe and twitch, even when they are flat bloodstains on the ground.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
Greymalkin swears: "By the black lung." He is desperate kicking squirming, flattened rats into the fire; stones is calm, but reminds greymalkin not to let any of their blood touch his skin

Rats can be heard fleeing outside; First light of dawn creeping through the cave mouth, revealing the gore.

Greymalkin is relieved, says they can go back (he has silently accepted Stones' plan to present the broken hewer to the captain as proof of The Ghoul's death)

The ratboar has fled but Stones knows from tales that if it is allowed to heal, it will come back stronger and meaner than before - he decided they have to hunt it down and kill it; Greymalkin would rather return to the other crusaders at their wagon caravan, but Stones is in charge (by virtue of being stronger).

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
Stones tells Greymalkin to have his oil ready, both carrying burning brands; both are jumpy, watching the stumps and dust for movement.

They hear snorting of the boar, and know it is near

Slowly build tension; the rats, the dust fog of day, the occasional splash of something in the water.

They arrive above the ruined spines of the bridge.

They hear the Squealer from the direction of the rotten woods; Stones grins. He is disappointed that it isn't a dog, but he draws his knife, and prepares for the killing (he is bubbly and excited; he likes killing, and he is good at it).

name: 13. The Finale
text:
"Stones and Greymalkin go on a 'boar hunt'; they lure the animal off the cliff over the destroyed bridge, then set fire to it with oil from the cave."
First part; 3 simples "gesture" combined in various ways
A texture (drums); a long description followed by a short one
A long pause, followed by a sudden action/magic (brass)
3 short hero (winds) or foe (strings) phrases
Followed by contrasting section; heroes/foes engage in regular combat
Accumulates into huge action by foes (strings and brass)
Sudden texture change (gong) is like the shivering of the earth; lustful hunger
Take-no-prisoners game for a while; violent action/magic (brass) and texture (drums)
Scene becomes faster, more compact, louder; heart thumping out-of-sync
Final rising, drawn-out action (brass)
A last trill from the foe (strings), then the heroes (winds)

name: Chrypt of the Rime-Coated Sails
text:
**In The Time of Dying, three chryptsackers hunt for a frozen ship of treasure, deep in a tomb on The Mountain of Graves.**

The Tomb is that of a Mail Flayer (a death knight servant in The Scratcher's church)

The main enemy in the chrypt are three giant undead birch trees disguised as masts (*Colony of the Birchmen*)

The birch trees attack when the company thaws the ship

name: Adoration of the Foe
text:
**The Chryptsackers hunt down and find the mailed Flayer's Tomb in the Foundation of the mountain, but are trapped inside.**

Introduce Birdsey, Othelmedir, and Fogg - the Chryptsackers

They interrogate a local tailor for the location of the tohmb

They explor the foundation for a while, and Othelmedir explains that they are searching for symbols of The Scratcher

They find the tohmb, but it is guarded by a leatherwyrm, and it traps them inside

name: Adoration - Chapter 1
text:
**The Chryptsackers hunt down and find the mailed Flayer's Tomb in the Foundation of the mountain, but are trapped inside.**

Introduce Birdsey, Othelmedir, and Fogg - the Chryptsackers

They interrogate a local Resurrector for the location of the tohmb

They explore the foundation for a while, and Othelmedir explains that they are searching for symbols of The Scratcher

They find the tohmb, but it is guarded by a leatherwyrm, and it traps them inside.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**Birdsey interrogates the tailor at his room in the middlemoss vault tavern, and gets him to reveal the map location.**

Start with various voices of people in the tavern; the man shuts them out, and mumbles to himself.

Sudden Birdsey holds knife to throat, has him breathing once for yes, twice for no - an interrogation

- Use to exposition on the map, the world, and the heroes; the resurrecter laughs, says the Foundation is too dangerous for a regular Chryptsacker

- “I saw where you buried the map…” Headstones rising from heath like the skin of a mummy, topped with tongue-colored heather fronds waving in the breath of the wind “Which grayve did you bury it in? I won’t dig them all - you resurrecter’s will have got any wares already.”

- Birdsey tisked. “You aught to be ashamed of yourself. Getting snuck by Killing Birds with Rocks. I’m lowly fodder. Othelmedir’s the linguist, the master mind. And Fogg - well, be glad I’m not Fogg. I’m just the little cook of the crew, though I must give myself credit where credit is due: I do throw a mean stone.”

Birdsey ruminates that Fogg will pull out his fingernails if he doesn’t kill the resurrecter - so he kills the man *(warn audience - this is the last chance to leave)*

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**The chryptsackers search for a way into the foundation, while trying to avoid undead or marauders.**

Skip digging up the map, just expositionize where they found it; jump to them descending into The Foundation

- They search for a Well leading down (*One small texture/description paragraph*)
- Othelmedir using Speech of the Dead to consult the nearby spirits, but most are too old and man this far down

*Think Morrowind ancestral tomb whispering sound (hint at enemy)*

Birdsey and Fogg banter about the true foe (the frozen ship, the mailed flayer; exposition)

Othelmedir repeats a sentence with variants at three different crossroads (*Surrender, surrender, surrender!*)

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**The chryptsackers find a well and start descending.**

(*Huge Textural description*)

- Stench grows around them(like the memory of primordial mildew; mildew that ate more than dead matter)
- A long, stairwell circling a huge hole in the floor, descending into blackness
- The characters of the stones changes, from mossy, to blackened with soot and deeply-shelved, covered in the dust of old bones

Describe echoes of their voices

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**They come out into The Foundation; Othelmedir tries to navigate a path to the tohmb.**

Initially, they only see looted hollows in the tunnel they find

Fogg and Birdsey converse again; Birdsey questions Otehl (show the changed atmosphere through their anxiety)

- Alternate between dialogue, and the echoes of their dialogue
- The echoes place different emphasis, making the words Wrathful or sarcastic

Gradually traverse the silent vaults, until they come to a corridor:

- Recesses line in shining blue plates
- Birsdey knows it hasn’t been looted, but Othelmedir warns him not to take the plates
- There are ten plates, which means that one is cursed, and they can’t know which

Fogg refuses to even look at the plates; in the end, they hurry past, feeling watched by the porcelain surfaces

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**They find the symbols of Sang, "The Scratcher", and eventually reach the door of the tohmb.**

Exiting corridor, they begin to notice symbols of The Scratcher (*Texture*)

Othelmedir and Birdsey consider how to open it (*Action*)

- Birdsey searching for a mechanism
- Othelmedir seeking magical aid

*Different elements compete for attention:*

- Serpent/Worm motifs line the walls (one of them is a dead one)
- The floor is littered with dust like that very thin dough (snakeskins/wormskins)
- Fogg is tense, senses something, says he hears it in the jungle of his bones
- Birdsey says they wouldn’t attract danger if he didn’t wear chimes everywhere they…

Suddenly something hisses in the door as Fogg leans against a pillar-stone...

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**The Door to the Tohmb creaks slowly open, but something else moves too.**

Big texture (feel-stench of the ancient air; the sound of the door like a wail; magical effects on the motifs)

*One of the motifs detaches from the wall (a Leatherwyrm; a worm that eats the rust and moss off of tombs; venomous skin)*

Just as they are about to head through, there is a second gasping/shuddering sound in the room

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**They fight the leatherwyrm, but are driven into the ancient chrypt.**

Describe the undead worm of leather skin and rust-colored, bony (*texture*)

Worm is enormous and untangles on the floor

Fogg holds the worm off, but it hurls them about, they are driven back through the door

The worm crushes a supporting pillar, and they are trapped (*Whooping and shrieking climax, heroes overwhelmed*)

name: The Heroics
text: Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text: (Usually 1 Chapter) Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**The chryptsackers descend, enter the frozen ship tohmb, and climb aboard the ship.**

The three, recover, Othelmedir groaning, Birdsey calls out; Othelmedir says he doesn't have the will to summon another light

Fogg says the path down feels open, starts leading them blindly; Shivering as they descend, breath misting

as they descend, the orb becomes superfluous anyway; a thin teal light-vapor suffuses the lower levels

Go into the tomb, and behold the ship on the revoir of ice; two steel stalactites in the ceiling; Minor texture (*drums*) showing a darker arena; night, stillness, death

Crossing the ice, see the dead below, composed, frozen faces blurred by ice; see another exit across the water (another way out)

Climb onto ship; Eerie, hypnotic, ritualistic scene; lull the audience into uncanny world of no precedent

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**The heroes explore for a way to thaw the treasure, and accidentally awaken the whole ship**

Find treasure piled around the masts, scratch marks in the wood, like it was dragged towards each mast

Birdsey complains it's freezing; Othelmedir says he suspects it is magical (hint at solution); Birdsey thinks it's mechanical

Find more in captains cabin along with frozen flayer and cleric acolytes (Othelmedir looking over some old books/scrolls/clothing/jewels; the lone treasure they get); The Mailed Flayer sits with his disciples in the captain’s quarters, frozen in time, treasure lying about him, frozen too

Othelmedir says the captain’s true dead, birdsey relaxes

They realize need to break to thaw the treasure

Fogg starts chipping at gold pile near a mast; Birdsey climbs some ledges

Othelmedir realizes that the ghost of a terrible mountain year of weather was captured, and siphoned; the blizzard that flayed the mountain is in wax right now; he needs to bring in the summer of storms

Othelmedir tries thinking of what signals summer (*they are all shivering throught scene; he finally realizes they need to sweat*)

Othelmedir stops short of getting started, fearing what a reversal might cause

The sound of Fogg panting brings him back, Othelmedir tells Fogg to stop

Fogg stops, and wipes his brow, throws the sweat to the ship (awakening the spring storm)

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**The storm rages in the chambers; the chryptsackers endure the onslaught.**

(*11 death notes*) Lightning and thunder flash, and the water changes to a turbulent sea; a bolt strikes Fogg on his temple, and stands glossy-eyed

Birdsey holding onto the face of the cliff for dear life as wind buffets him

A wave strikes the ship sidelong, sends it reeling; Fogg grabs a rail, then grabs Othelmedir's injured arm

Treasure flies over the gunnels as another wave crashes

Finally Fogg manages to secure an elbow on one of the rails, Birdsey wraps himself in thick vines, they hold on as the storm thrashes

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**The chryptsackers recover while the rain pours down; they plan to leave by the opposite exit.**

Everyone checks in; Fogg and Othelmedir are in pain, but can move; Birdsey is allright.

Birdsey says he can climb over to the exit.

Fogg sees some scattered treasure still; Othelmedir says they should check the helm.

Wonder how they will get across; notice the bodies floating in the water.

Birdsey suggests they make a raft from the bodies; Fogg says they'll just use ship wood; suggests the table in the captain's quarters.

Suddenly, hear a scraping sound from the masts...

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**The birch trees awaken, moving about to gather their scattered gold; one brushes Othelmedir.**

All three watch the three birchmen awaken, star moving around the deck, sweeping gold back into piles.

Othelmedir recognizes them (*Exposition*) holds up a finger to be silent.

The heroes start trying to creep away, toward the edge of the water.

Bidsey drops a rock, one of the trees swivels, but they are unnoticed.

Fogg and Othelmedir look over the edge, at the water, in the rain, they shrug, prepare to jump.

One of the trees walks across a line, starts dragging it, it catches Fogg's foot and sends him toppling with a crash (*cue for finale*)

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**The Birchmen attack; Fogg, Othelmedir, and Birdsey try to fight, but are overmatched; Othelmedir saves them by invoking the parching heat of summer.**

3 simple "gestures" combined:

- A texture (*drums*); a long description followed by a short one
- A long pause, followed by a sudden action/magic (*brass*)
- Othelmedir shouts out a warning of what they are, starts thinking

Fogg hacks at one of the trees, wood chips flying, but sword gets stuckl

The other two birchmen converge; he releases his sword, draws morningstar, and retreats.

Othelmedir still thinking, then figures out how the bring about summer; remembers it as the summer of the fullday sun; summon a yellow globe

Sudden crackle, timbers instantly drying, steam evaporating, horribly hot; like a shiver of the earth

Fogg reacts quickly, tears his sword from the dry tree, chops one down; crashes to the deck

Pick up pace, Birdsey shouts from the shore for them to run; Fogg hard-pressed by the remaining two trees; steam is slowly lifting

Othelmedir hears a crackle, turns and sees the captains and his pirates burning, ship catching fire; floor suddenly feels hot, realizes it is burning beneath him; hears straining, sees a burning line pulled taught by one of the birch masts; suddenly snaps, clobbers Othelmedir's head, sends him splashing over the side

(*Ending*) Fogg swims between the rows of floating dead men, dragging Othelmedir (who can't) onto the shore of the exit tunnel, the meet with Birdsey, watch the ship burn and sink and leave empty-handed (except Othelmedir managed to grab something)

name: 25000 Sugar Lumps
text:
**A Satire of Substack, TikTok, and Video-over-Reading:**

Tells the story of two ant colonies: TipTap and The Stack.

The ants of TipTap discover the juice of a special fruit, which they can digest instead of the hard nuts and other food.

The juice is soon discovered by all major ant colonies.

A group of rebel ants leaves TipTap and found a new colony under a Stack of Stones.

The Queen of the new colony promises that there will be no ant juice in The Stack of Stones. This is a lie.

As Stack Juice is introduced, the Stack of Stones becomes like every other colony.

name: Single Act
text:

Tells the story of two ant colonies: TipTap and The Stack.

The ants of TipTap discover the juice of a special fruit, which they can digest instead of the hard nuts and other food.

The juice is soon discovered by all major ant colonies.

A group of rebel ants leaves TipTap and found a new colony under a Stack of Stones.

name: Single Chapter
text:
**A Satire of Substack, TikTok, and Video-over-Reading:**

Tells the story of two ant colonies: TipTap and The Stack.

The ants of TipTap discover the juice of a special fruit, which they can digest instead of the hard nuts and other food.

The juice is soon discovered by all major ant colonies.

A group of rebel ants leaves TipTap and found a new colony under a Stack of Stones.

The Queen of the new colony promises that there will be no ant juice in The Stack of Stones. This is a lie.

As Stack Juice is introduced, the Stack of Stones becomes like every other colony.

name: Setup
text:
All had once been well for Ant Princess Christina, until the discovery of the Ant Juice.

Introduce Colony, Ant Queen Suzie Chew, industry.

Describe Ant Princess and her happy life.

- A face and voice that could sell pyramid schemes

Describe the Ant Juice; what it did to the colony.

- “And the colony of TipTap build no new tunnels. And many ants starved, for they had stopped gathering food. Yet the ants who did not starve drooled with joy, for they had endless TipTap juice to drink.”
- Describe how ants act when they drink Ant Juice (TikTok trends).
- Come up with a clear, vivid sign of full degeneracy (for the finale)

For a time, princess tried to convince the queen that "Something is Rotten in the state of TipTap." Ant queen won't listen.

On of the royal advisors Beefoid O'Keith (Hamish Mckenzie) suggests a new colony. A Colony far from the Ant Juice, where the only fruits grow far too high in the trees (*Foreshadow*).

The princess bites her antenna; never before had she considered rebellion, yet never had the stakes been so high.

name: Conflict
text:
The outcast ants travelled far, and suffered much (how? Stream crossing? anteaters?).

One of the ants who comes was an explorer who discovered Ant Juice. He tries to bring some, but isn't allowed.

Finally come to a huge stack of stones. They dug underneath, to a subsurface series of tunnels.

By this time, the ants are very small in number, with no food, and they know they must build.

They start working, building tunnels, making the new kingdom of Stack of Stones beautiful.

- Christina is crowned queen, has to lose her wings; has to work extra hard to lay eggs, and to manage the colony - hard early days, but with the help of her advisors, she works tirelessly

- The Queen is Initially firm: to her, it is a matter of principle: Ants having the means to be their best

- Repeated phrase: “The Stack is different. A colony of willful ants. A colony of hardworking ants. A colony of builders, and dreamers, and not those daydreamers of TipTap.”

The fruit that makes ant juice is seen high above, but the ants are forbidden to go there.

Queen sets up reward system of food for ants that work hard, giving them independence (parallel Substack model).

However, the explorer ant keeps trying to convince her to bring in ant juice, says its best for the colony.

Queen is initially firm, but hears from other migrants that her colony is struggling - "ants are leaving the castle." - going to other colonies (Sign of Apparent defeat).

name: Drive
text:
The Queen finally gives in, and the explorer reveals he has already had some samples of the fruit brought down, he can test them immediately; the queen gives in:

- "My queen," said Beefoid, "I beg you not to do this. Bringing Ant Juice into Stack of Stones will be the opening of Pandora's Box. Not ant would compass the removal of Ant Juice if we decided it had been a mistake."

- “We’ve made the juice better. It won’t be TipTap Juice here. It will be the juice of our wonderful Stack of Stones. Stack Juice. Just another option, beyond the regular produce of the colony. You have the solemn promise of your queen: the greatest builders and dreamers, in our subsurface corner of the rainforest, under this stack of stones, shall still receive the greatest reward.”

Initially, the ants are extactic; brings a new surge of productivity, and a new wave of ant immigrants.

Gradually, however, the novelty of the ant juice starts to wear off; ants still drink ant juice, but it no longer seems to result in more productive output

- The ants living in the subsurface stack of stones wanted desperately to bring in new colonists. The queen even posted a generous reward: 25,000 grains of sugar to the ant who could bring the most colonists over from the warren of TipTap. Yet of those who came, few stayed. “Why should we drink Stack Juice?” said they. “Our formula was perfect already. Your juice - if it isn’t inferior - is certainly no better. Your juice is exactly the same as everywhere else.”
- (*This is the spiritual Crisis*)

name: Finale
text:
Final scene is the explorer and the queens advisors brainstorming ideas for how to use ant juice, or make their flavor more appealing that the other colonies

- In each instance, it has been tried before
- Meanwhile the queen is sitting quietly, drinking ant juice herself, not conscious of her own movements, reflecting on the flavor of her juice, and the state of colony, trying to convince herself that all is well
- Suddenly the queen realizes that all the other councilors have stopped talking
- The queen looks up, sees them staring at her, and realizes she was unconsciously making the juice sign of degeneracy (boom. knockout.)

name: The Lake of the Lost
text: Othelmedir is upheld by marauders while crossing a bridge over a lake of undead. The bandits try to steal his wagon and abduct him, but are forced to join him when the undead rise from the lake and walk on the water.

name: Single Act
text: Othelmedir is upheld by marauders while crossing a bridge over a lake of undead. The bandits try to steal his wagon and abduct him, but are forced to join him when the undead rise from the lake and walk on the water.

name: Single Chapter
text: Othelmedir is upheld by marauders while crossing a bridge over a lake of undead. The bandits try to steal his wagon and abduct him, but are forced to join him when the undead rise from the lake and walk on the water.

name: Setup
text:
**Introduce the Setting, the Hero, and the Plot.**

Start with the DISTURBANCE

- The break in ordinary routine.

- The 'Call to Adventure'.

- The *Motion*.

Introduce the HERO.

Introduce the STORY WORLD

- What is the Hero's life?

Introduce the INTIMIDATING OPPORTUNITY.

(*Possibly*) Hint at the FOE.

Cross the threshold into *Conflict*.

name: Conflict
text:
**Create problems and enemies.**

Entire time, the Hero is trying to solve the main PROBLEM.

- What is the worst thing that can happen?
- What is the worst info the HERO can receive?

Establish a TIMER.

Introduce the main FOE:

- Make them a *single* person (or, personify).
- Make them stronger than the HERO.
- *Why do you love them?*

Define DEATH; the threat to the HERO (physical, or psychological, or professional).

Establish a SUPPRESSED CONFLICT (subtext).

Introduce a APPARENT DEFEAT, leading into *Drive*.

name: Drive
text:
**Draw the opponents closer together.**

Starting at the low point, introduce a LAST CHANCE.

Make the PROBLEM progressively worse:

- Stretch the tension.
- Raise the stakes.
- Increase the pace.

Show CHARACTER EMOTION:

- The HERO's desperation.

Have another setback, leading the HERO to a SPIRITUAL CRISIS.

name: Finale
text:
**End the story with a Knockout blow.**

Give the HERO a SELF-REVELATION.

Bring all the threads together.

Have the HERO overcome or succumb-to the challenge.

- Have the FOE do the same.

Bring the HERO's PROBLEM to a CLIMAX.

Show the new EQUILIBRIUM.

name: Vigyazz
text: You (An unnamed knight) seek a **Silver Ring**, one of five. Lord **Wraithcotte** was its last owner, and so you travel to the ruin of his estate, deep in the **Landmine Forest.**

name: Adoration of the Foe
text: Introduce the unnamed knight, the quest for the Silver Ring, and the Landmine Forest. Reach the Wraithcotte Estate, and get inside.

name: Adoration - Chapter 1
text: Introduce the unnamed knight, the quest for the Silver Ring, and the Landmine Forest. Reach the Wraithcotte Estate, and get inside.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**Introduce the quest for the Silver Ring, and the Unnamed Knight.**

Opening: begin with 4ish lines of dialogue; an echo of memory, the last words of the necromancer Othelmedir, describing the location of the ring

The memory echo fades, as the trunks close in;

Necromancy will not work in the Landmine Forest; the poison within the mines makes the dead sleep. If Othelmedir’s Summoned Golem survived your encounter with Old Twothroats, it will perish once you enter the forest.

"You check your equipment one final time:"

Bold text: 2x Weapon / armor / item name, then description

Use second item description to hint at the forest threats

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**Not long after entering, hear a chattering noise (interaction one); later, see a giant slug making its way between the trees.**

“The Late Poet” - skeleton friend, wears a plague mask - Description

- Sitting at the edge of a wooded gorge, composing a poem

- NPC based on Snowyteller (Come to strike my heart a mortal blow, HO HEE, HO HEE) (Hyperlink him)

You may stab the skeleton through its ribs, at the spot where its heart would be. If you do, the skeleton clasps its hands over its chest, stumbles drmatically backward, and plummets apart down a cliff, fate unknown

Second Page: continuing on through the wood (*light, unique texture description of slime trail*) hear a slithing sound

- The knight find a hollow stump and climbs inside
- Peers through a crack, and sees the slug sidle past: squelch, squelch, squelch...

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**You mount a ridge looking over the moonlit forest, and see the distant estate.**

Unique description of the forest; focus on the distant signs of the estate (Wraithcotte Estate)

- As night ascends, the moon shines full over the canopy. The forest floor is dappled in bright silver light.
- Remains of a sign stick up out of the soil: "Vigyázz!"
- a thought crosses mind: There are few bastions left for the un-dead within the Landmine Forest.
- Venture on

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**After journeying for several hours, and narrowly avoiding a slug, you come upon a place of rest; while there, another slug attacks.**

Finds a cave formed from the roots of three trees, while high hedges form walls around; go inside to investigate

“**The Song-singing Fungus:** If you sing a few lines from any song, this long-frilled mushroom will sing the rest. Its clear, resonant voice fills you with peace. You may sleep beside it to restore *missing sanity?*.”

Character muses internally on his question

**Page 2:**

Though the fungus's song seemed sweet at first, it takes on a deadly note, and the hero finds himself sinking into a wakeless sleep

Man realizes he has lain for several days by the mushroom, he is starving, the roots have grown over his armor and sword (*Huge Texture*)

Throw bug lantern; it breaks; the torchbugs sting the fungus and kill it, but scatter like embers from stomped coals (the hero will have to continue on in the dark).

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**The knight breaks through the wood onto the estate; he sneaks past the old guard skeletons to reach the door:**

Describe sword leading the way from the treeline; Describe the ruin of The Wraithcotte Estate

- Estate seems like it is all gabled windows, turrets, stone outbuilding mausoleums rising from the overgrown bluestem grass
- All the windows are broken and boarded from the inside; but in each window, the jagget skirt of glass remaining is a different color
- Despite being a wide building, the manor has a tall, crooked, slightly swaying aspect to it; like a hunched old man, usnteady on his feet


- The CONSTANT SOUND from the woods is muted here
- The air has lost the earthy petrichor smell, replaced by the dying-aristocracy smell of churches and courthouses

The knight sneaks past the Old Guard Skeletons, sees the recess where the front door would be, but stops and crouches in the tall grass.

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**Patrolling Undead**

*Sound of an enemy approaching through the wood, in a patrol, then passing around the other side:*

"Repeated Phrase."

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**The knight Reaches the Door with a Face, and figures out how to pass it.**

Briefly describe the door.

Weapons will prove useless against the Door with a Face. You can try setting off your landmine, but it has no effect.

- The only way to pass The Door with a Face is to answer all four of its riddles correctly.

- The answers are: 1. A Bouquet, 2. Depression, 3. The Mouse Did It, 4. I Offer You My Sense of Smell.

After getting through, rotten boards give way, and you plunge into darkness.

name: The Heroics
text: Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text: (Usually 1 Chapter) Give the heroes a last-ditch chance; present the finale.

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**Introduce the Sightless Pit:**

The dust settles about you, and you are surprised you can see it

- Finds that the area still has soft red light
- Mushroom has left red-glowing spores on the knight’s body, allowing him to see in the manor
- Rise, feeling perhaps a few broken ribs, and start down a hall of rough stones

The Sightless Pit:

- Deep and unlit; the halls beneath the Wraithcotte Estate have uncounted floors, floor-below-floor, and there are more passages leading down than up.

Skeletons roam the halls in programmed routes, you will have to navigate them, drawing their attention will trigger the whole swarm to attack

The wine-barrels that line the walls are all hollow and empty. Each one has the following icon on its surface: TODO symbol

At first the icon seems harmless; you soon realize, however, that if you stare at any icon for a long time, you feel an acrid taste of sour wine in your throat, and your head starts to swim as if drunk. Thereafter, you keep your eyes averted.

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**The hero continues through the sightless pit, finds a magic potion, and escapes via a well.**

Hero finds a magic potion/object that will allow him to fight the final foe (poetry? a mirror?)

Part 9: Hall of Portraits:

“Away-facer” - a figure encased in smoke that is always facing away from you, even if try to meet their eye

“Man with frozen body” only his head is free, the rest of his body is encased in a block of ice; he screams

“The woman with backwards eyes” she sees inside you

At the end of the hall of portraits, the well room (the woman lowers you by a bucket into the well, but when she lifts it out again, you are on the first floor of the estate; and immediately attacked)

USE TITUS GROAN FOR DIALOGUE: “They killed my animals.”

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**The Bell Ringer:**

The Bell Ringer: enter a fight with a skeleton, has a bell for a hand, a cone around its mouth, and two clawed feet

- The bell ringer will constantly intone a repeated phrase, and ring his bell during the fight
- If the bell ringer has not been destroyed by the time of his 11th ring, you will fall unconscious, the tolling ringing in your dreams; he will crush your body with his feet
- Avoid his kicks, and strike at his bell-ringing hand whenever he pauses to breath in before speaking
- If you sever his hand at the wrist, he will be stunned. You can then dispatch him with ease.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**The knight has a moment of relief, then sets off into the house:**

With bellringer dead, eerie silence settles over the house, not even the rats to break it

After catching your breath, you venture on

Much like the pits, the mansion of the estate is vast and layered, floor-after-floor that seem to be made only of corner-halls and corner-rooms, attic surmounting attic, stairs leading to narrower stairs.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**The knight searches the mansion for the ring.**

Describe: rooms in the mansion, feeling of being stalked; you try to peel the boards from one of the windows, if only to see the sky again, but no matter how many boards you tear away there are more behind.

Action: describe how to navigate, then list some objects which may be helpful in the return journey; if you find the ring

- You can try leaving chalk marks, but something keeps erasing them. The only way to navigate is to leave a tooth (yours or someone else's) at each explored passage. Whoever or whatever erases the chalk marking leaves the teeth alone.

Describe: after many hours (impossible to retrace steps), entry into the Grand Arena room (Cathedral), followed by entry of the enemy

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**A final battle with the last Wraithcotte:**

Briefly describe Merilka Wraithcotte:

- Vampire Queen (think of Akivasha; beautiful on the outside, but rotten on the inside; an abortion of mortality)
- Give brief lore background (last of the Wraithcottes)
- Her name means: Rose of the Sacred Barque

For final battle: write it like a dark-souls boss guide

- To beat X, you will need to drink the potion from The Sightless Pit. Doing so will give you The Red King’s Curse - which will make your skin reflective. This curse is permanant, and lasts after death, but it is preferable to dying now.
- Merilka will try to skewer you with her two rapier, which are thin as needles, and sharp enough to puncture steel. She usually attacks in X pattern. When she open her flank at the end of X, you have a small window to strike her hip. If you miss your window, you will have to endure another round of her pattern.
- The first serious wound you inflict on Marilka will send her into hysterical weeping. She will go berzerk. Now her rapiers stab in twice as quickly as before. She is slowed by her enjury, however, and you are able to keep a little space between you now.
- After a relentless flurry, she will need to rest and recover. Use this as a chance to doff your helm and unbuckle your breastplate; they are useless against her attacks anyway. When she blinks away her tears and attacks again, your reflection will startle her; as she sees her own tearstained eyes. This is your only opportunity; stab her Through the heart with your bitter blade.

On the ground, alongside Merika Wraithcotte's body, a choice lies before you. Taking the Silver Ring from her finger, you may either place it into a pocket, or slip it over your own.

*In the Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.*

name: The Spit of Mool
text:
**The Cracked Cup Knight must retrieve The Spit of Mool, an old necromancer-toad-centipede, from the rusted chrypt where the monster lies dreaming.**

First part: CCK breaks into the rusted tomb.

- Reflects on his need to repay Othelmedir, and his qualms of grave-robbing
- Becomes accustomed to his newly-resurrected body, and its limits
- He is captured by the rusted clockwork apes of Mool

Second Part: CCK awakens deep within The Heather Vault, the inner sanctum. He escapes with Mool's spit.

- CCK is caged in rusted bars, his sword stolen, the automoton apes dance around.
- CCK breaks out by luring an aped over and breaking off its tail, which he uses to pry the door
- CCK grabs sword and black drool from the floor, and runs.
- Lost in The Heather Vault, CCK must escape back to outer door, while hunted by the sleepwalking necromancer-toad (Mool is beyond his skill).

text:
**The Cracked Cup Knight must retrieve The Spit of Mool, an old necromancer-toad-centipede, from the rusted chrypt where the monster lies dreaming.**

- Becomes accustomed to his newly-resurrected body, and its limits

- Becomes accustomed to his newly-resurrected body, and its limits
- He is captured by the rusted clockwork apes of Mool

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**The Cracked Cup Knight breaks into Mool's Chrypt, and prepares himself for the task ahead.**

Open with CCK using pendulum hammer to break down door

Steps inside, considers the hall; feels some pleasure at the destroyed tomb (the *broken* tomb), then suppresses it

describe longsword, armor, shield

Considers the quest before him (Othelmedir's words)

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**The CCK makes his first foray in the grave by stealth; he knows he must remain hidden; he sees the automaton apes.**

Describe the habits and manners of the automonton apes; their *industry.*

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**The CCK arrives at the huge watery trench in the Chrypt, and watches the apes**

Describe the rusted tomb mine; they are carving out niches, gathering crystals

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**The CCK watches as the apes perform their labors in the tomb, and wonders at his purpose there**

Routine of the apes, feeding crystals into lake of jellyfish (Why? foreshadowing)

CCK has inner qualms about grave-robbing

Action of the gearslave apes; they are massaging crystals in the walls, digging/shaping niches, rubbing wax over bones.

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**The Knight is discovered as he tries to flee the gulch; he fights toward an exit.**

Knight makes a little progress, comes to narrow end of gulch; sees bridges.

One of the apes lands beside CCK. He is discovered.

CCK starts trying to fight his way out, toward one suspension bridge leading down.

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**The CCK *feels* the approach of Mool:**

Huge description of approahcing foulness (but don't actually describe Mool; real fear comes from the unknown)

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**The Knight knows Mool is beyond him; he tries to flee, but is trapped; he hits the water and sinks.**

The knight is trapped on a hanging-bridge between two sides of a chasm lined in vaults

He jumps, and falls unconscious when he sinks into the water.

name: The Heroics
text:
**The Cracked Cup Knight must retrieve The Spit of Mool, an old necromancer-toad-centipede, from the rusted chrypt where the monster lies dreaming.**

Second Part: CCK awakens deep within The Heather Vault, the inner sanctum. He escapes with Mool's spit.

- CCK is caged in rusted bars, his sword stolen, the automaton apes dance around.
- CCK breaks out by luring an aped over and breaking off its tail, which he uses to pry the door
- CCK grabs sword and black drool from the floor, and runs.
- Lost in The Heather Vault, CCK must escape back to outer door, while hunted by the sleepwalking necromancer-toad (Mool is beyond his skill).

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text:
**The Cracked Cup Knight must retrieve The Spit of Mool, an old necromancer-toad-centipede, from the rusted chrypt where the monster lies dreaming.**

Second Part: CCK awakens deep within The Heather Vault, the inner sanctum. He escapes with Mool's spit.

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**The CCK awakens deep within The Heather Vault; he is caged; the apes prepare a pot to "boil his bones free of his skin."**

Describe knight waking up to the feeling of something wrapping his hands, and a memory of the lady: "I will hold your hands."

Describe the Heather vaults, tombs lined with growing heather, apes cultivating it

The Knight is trapped in a cage

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**The gearslave apes hoot, and begin to pile more heather on the burning pile under the cauldron; the knight considers how to use the one before him.**

Knight notices how the ape's tail is built; a single, heavy strut of metal, but loosely connected.

The Apes revel, heaping the flames higher.

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**The CCK rips the ape's tail from its body, pries open the door, grabs his sword, and flees.**

Knight breaks the door open with a squeal, huge battle of apes chasing, he tries to retrieve spit but fails and flees.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**The CCK manages to escape and hide from the apes; he then searches for an escape.**

Knight hides, the apes run past, he starts sneaking through the vaults

Bold text of possible rooms you may hide in (list of locations)

- Ald Medas Shrine: Here you crouch among the splintered piles of ten thousand exhausted magical wands.
- Big Eye's Cave: Seven ivory eggs encased in amber stud the ceiling of this room.
- Selgon: This pillared corridor reminds you of an apprentice knight you once escorted through a similar sepulcher.
- Index Chamber: A snake of yellow light slithers constantly through the air between two pilons in this room. The shifting illumination makes this a surprisingly-good place to hide.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**The CCK finds Mool's Inner Sancrum within the Vaults; he retrieves the spit, but draws the grot's wrath by killing his Jellyfish.**

Knight finds Mool's inner experiment chambers (spit, jellyfish, unfinished rusted automatons, crystals)

He finds his shield lying on a table; wonders how much some of the other valuables would be worth at an Emerald House (a temple of Lady Horsehair)

He slaughters the pristine jellyfish in an uncontrolled moment (he is cracked)

Hears a deep, bassy gurgle from elsewhere in the vaults

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**The CCK gets lost in The Heather Vault; he runs and hides from Mool; he finds a possible escape, and just manages before seeing Mool's shadow.**

Knight flees, hides in the heather, manages to escape the searching centipede-toad-necromancer

Mention that the only other time the knight had seen one of the Grot was as a shriveled, mummified thing

- In one of the tents of The Coffin Rollers which roll across the road of graves

name: The Funeral on The Road of Graves
text:
**The Knight and The Lady encounter a sad whore while trying to escape from their hunters.**

They enter the small village of The Cat in the Jar.

- Pursued by assassins from rival family; still don't know why.

They meet the consumptive whore, Magpie (Magpie Food).

Magpie dies before they can save her

name: Planets Sole Act
text:
**The Knight and The Lady encounter a sad whore while trying to escape from their hunters.**

They enter the small village of The Cat in the Jar.

- Pursued by assassins from rival family; still don't know why.

They meet the consumptive whore, Magpie (Magpie Food).

Magpie dies before they can save her.

name: Planets Sole Chapter
text:
**The Knight and The Lady encounter a sad whore while trying to escape from their hunters.**

They enter the small village of The Cat in the Jar.

- Pursued by assassins from rival family; still don't know why.

They meet the consumptive whore, Magpie (Magpie Food).

Magpie dies before they can save her.

name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**The CCK and his Blue Lady are hiding at a Lodging House in the burg of Ripe, when they are attacked by assassins from Wiege Family.**

CCK and Lady discuss the recent deaths of her family; they realize they need to get out of town

- Use to introduce character of lady
- Quiet opening; they know they are in danger

Assassin's break into Lady's chamber:

- Open to huge scene; textural description and major battle; Violence and Sheer Terror
- "Attack while defending by raising The Pinecone Crest Shield between you and your lady, and your attackers; then stab out repeatedly with quick jabs of your Polished Steel Longsword."

Knight is knocked from window into courtyard, where more assassins await

- more action; back/forth fighting
- Enemy/Foe seems to take the victory; *Experimental, craggy rhythm; clashing keys; pulsing drums*
- CCK hears lady scream, sees her running along out catwalk, assassin starts to chase
- CCK grabs crossbow, takes aim, fires; Lady almost dies, but CCK saves her (With a huge blare, hero strikes final blow)

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady meet Magpie on The Road of Groves; they enter The Cat in the Jar.**

CCK and Lady travel to small village of The Cat in the Jar.

- Sight of Ripe (build along one side of a Mesa where it overlooks the Vast Lake of Tomatoes); finally turn a corner of the cliffs, city is gone (*Last sight of "home"*)
- They are following the road of Graves, many miles from the city
- They discuss what they will do to escape (fear they will be found again; *set up later conflict*)

Introduce Magpie the whore:

- She ingratiates herself with the CCK by discussing how the town is astir lately (*set up tragedy, or unrealistic desire*)
- Town believe the moon is going to crash into town every time it is a hunter's moon (such as tonight)
- It makes the cats howl and stir

They come within sight of The Cat in the Jar

- Dreamy and idyllic, but with one noticeable dark spot; make the town homely, but not safe

Magpie clasps the CCK's hand and bids farewell

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**A tiny scene between knight and lady, thinking they are being manipulated by Magpie.**

The lady mentions she doesn't trust magpie, thinks magpie could be a spy.

They have just been turned away from another lodge:

- Describe Village through ACTION; absolutely *haunted by cats;* everyone obsessed with cats; life revolves around cats


- They have built a bridge over a deep ravine, where glass bubble-enclosures have been built inside the ravine walls
- "Your search for lodging has not been fruitful."

Knight wonders at his lady; doesn't think she could be jealous; she knows his fidelity to his oaths

End with very brief description of a resting place they find; though they did not intend to, they stay for several days due to bad weather (humor here)

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**The CCK and his Blue Lady start to enjoy life in town; they spend more time getting to know Magpie, and start to feel comfortable.**

Start with expositionary description:

- Despite the poor weather, there is a certain cheer in town after the threat of this hunter's moon has proven yet another false one.
- Magpie brings you basket of apples.
- CCK does another service with his steel, which wins him favor of the town.

Describe a Melted Wax Golem (make it huge; foreshadow Alsoburg);

- Lady dialogue near the golem; her daughter asked for such a servant once; she admits to seeing her daughter's face more often
- Abruptly, lady says she doesn't think that prostitute is a spy

Give list of actions; things the CCK can do to make their lives better

- One action; leave your slash in the wooden totem of the snake, beside the lavender-painted house on the east side of town; other knight have come before...
- Another action; destroy the scarecrow in farmer X's field, which has attained sentience; one strike will only make it cackle, so be sure to hack it down with at least two blows
- Eventually can learn of a cottage that rents rooms; no sign, but colloqially, The Cast Iron Inn, for the enameled iron pots that decorate its long fireplace mantle.
- "The Inn opens after dark, sir."; but Magpie gets you and lady a room (magpie coughing again)

Prostitute says she dreamed once of being a great lady; in the Court of the Leather Dragon

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**Magpie dies, just as the CCK and Lady start to feel at home.**

Open with super-sad, plainly given, depressing line

- Something like: "It was not until your third night in The Cat in the Pot that you realized how near to the end was Magpie's tuberculosis.

Description: CCK and Lady go to Magpie's "House"

- It is a small, wet cave by the river - enough woolen sheets to keep the men comfortable for an hour, but not for a whole evening; it is freezing
- Magpie coughing; blood on her thin cloth

Some dialogue from Magpie; she worries because there is no temple that can perform her rites; the nearest is in Ripe

Description: The lady takes hold of magpie's hands while Magpie coughs

Reflection: the knight thinks they must get her to a temple, but is worries what his lady will say

Later: as Magpie is resting, Lady has dialogue where she says they must return; granting Magpie rites and finding a stone on the road

In the morning, Magpie is cold and dead

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**A battle of mystical, magical, strange character; the hero(s) fight in an upside-down reality.**

Open with four huge notes of action (*four-brass motif*)

Heroes begin desperate, insane ploy to succeed; everything exaggerated

Heroes and Foes clash

- Comic/Lighthearted in places; Foes treating heroes like a joke

Chaotic, willy-wonka scenery - a world gone mad

Build to climactic, *epic* battle

- Echo moments of Mars; show the hero in their old glory for a moment

Foe seems to achieve victory, mocks the pitiful hero(s)

- Heroes suddenly strike out in suicidal, self-destructive final blow

End with Foe sinking quietly into death

**The CCK and Lady are ambushed while trying to bury Magpie along the ROG.**

Open with bold text and huge, four-note, action-description.

When they go to bury her on the road of graves, the assassins catch up to them

- Insane, exaggerated combat; the ass ride in on horses with bows
- use ACTION; describe sequence of steps, lady must defend CCK across the bridge with the bubble globes, they throw one assassin over

One assassin laughing madly as he leaps in behind

- One item retrieved: one of the set of 23 Alabaster Masks (which one? Grieving Father Mask?) which allows the wearer to assume a false identity.
- While wearinging Grieving Father Mask: gain skin like leather, and a ristance to lesser cuts; but fire will burn you to a crisp.

chaotic, willy-wonka combat, when Suddenly the CCK manages some kind of last-minute save; they flee

- Rites are incomplete; Magpie died with finding rest (save for later)

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**The Knight and his Lady leave The Cat in the Pot; with Magpie's soul-fate unknown; fleeing from their pursuers, destination unknown.**

Open scene with the village fading behind them (leave uncertain which direction at first; only later reveal that it is far from Ripe)

- Leave Magpie's fate uncertain at first too
- Knight and lady take to the road, a sadder and a wiser pair.

Reflection: Knight wonders what might have been for Magpie, had her fate been different

Dialogue: Lady says that Magpie wanted to be an elegant lady in a noble court; then says she's glad Magpie died without knowing what that was like

Description: They come upon the toll-gate of the ten-plates ghost

- There are a pair of guards there with spears

Guard questions the knight (Dialogue), but decides that the knight is just escorting his sorrowful father

- Warns the knight that steel is needed if they're going to the western cities of the lake of Tomatoes, the cities are a place of violence

End description of skeleton trees casting their shadows over the terse grass, and the eyes of the lady's palrey gazing over the lake (where the story started)

*In the time of dying...*

name: The Walkers of Whiteeye
text:
**The CCK and his Blue Lady must cross The Whiteeye Waste with the help of an escort; The Walkers.**

They join the escort and "Fight the Blight".

Along the way, they are hunted by a "Hearse": a flying bat-dragonfly monster which hunts those it can see at night.

name: Mother Goose Act
text:
**The CCK and his Blue Lady  must c ross The Whiteeye Waste with the help of an escort; The Walkers.**

They join the escort and "Fight the Blight".

Along the way, they are hunted by a "Hearse": a flying bat-dragonfly monster which hunts those it can see at night.

name: Mother Goose Chapter
text:
**The CCK and his Blue Lady must c ross The Whiteeye Waste with the help of an escort; The Walkers.**

They join the escort and "Fight the Blight".

Along the way, they are hunted by a "Hearse": a flying bat-dragonfly monster which hunts those it can see at night.

Structured like a Long Chase (*Meltdown of the Orb; Mad Max Fury Road*)

SHOW Transition - Lake of Tomatoes to Middlemoss

- **Main Motive** is the Transition - Embody in the landscape; show a gradual change from familiar

name: 1 - Prelude
text:
**The CCK and his Blue Lady are leaving The Lake of Tomatoes.**

Open the Main Motive of *Transition* - The CCK and Lady are on the Road of Graves

- Encapsulate the main motive in a *Tune* - the song of the Hearse - the sound of hunger

- They look back for last time on the lake; three distinct textures (*two flutes and bassoon*)

Lady worries about what lies ahead

- CCK crossed Whiteye in a caravan once, but that was decades ago, when he sought the end of the road (*Once upon a time*)

- CCK worries about crossing without aid; wonders how well the road is maintained

- Lady mentions a specific monster she knows lives on the mesas around the lake; asks CCK if such a monster haunts this place too

- CCK mention that, no doubt, they will find shelter

Road *narrows* as they come to the edge of civilized land (gradually zoom in)

- They pass between the six black obelisks that mark the edge of the lake

name: 2 - Opening Conflict
text:
**The CCK and Blue enter The Whiteye Wastes themselves, and quickly become lost in the trackless road.**

CCK and Lady try to venture themselves, but are turned back

Hint at the Hearse (but don't show it yet); They hear a rumbling song, like the growling of a stomach

The CCK and Lady are attacked by *Dust Dogs* - blighted mutts that camoflage themselves in the grey-black dust, and infect people with blight

"Lady Horsehair created The Whiteye Waste as a barrier - a doorway- between Middlemoss and The Lake of Tomatoes

- Wasn't born in a barn, and shut the door tightly to keep out Gallbladder and his disgusting rifraff

name: 3 - Mentor
text:
**The CCK and his Lady are saved by a mysterious caravan - The Walkers - who are not friendly to begin with.**

Encounter the walkers (embody with one specifically, the one who is poisoned)

- The walkers threaten to disrobe the lady; knight tries to fight, but is helpless
- Lady disrobes herself - seeming ancient and serene - knight initially turns his eye away, knowing it is not right for him to see her
- One of the walker's grabs her, but the commander of the walkers (Mother) stops him

The walkers describe the dust

- They agree to escort the pair

name: 4 - First Transition
text:
**The leader of the Walkers introduces herself, and explains the Waste.**

Introduce the leader, Vettel

The Walkers explain the waste, and what lies ahead.

- Black-Grey dust has covered the road in the waste - they must use an astrolabe to navigate
- They are aloof/mysterious about what lies ahead, and about the stomach song - the song of the Hearse

name: 5 - New Lover
text:
**The CCK and Lady find a friend among The Walkers, who explains The Whiteye Waste, and the danger of the Hearse.**

"Over the next several weeks..."

- getting used to the waste
- CCK and lady befriend one of the Walkers - the one who gets poisoned and will die

"If you retrieved it from Birchbark the Necromance, you can use the Sorrowful Father Mask to gain the captain's trust as well, though she is fated to be no person's friend."

Finally they hear the rumble again, and ask what it is:

- Dialogue: Friendly walker is angry and storms away at first (*cymbal crash*)

The friendly walker eventually returns and explains the Hearse:

- The Dragonfly has fed on blighted blood (something like mothra, but with a long stinger-tail, and a hundred shimmering wings)

- A humming sound, but enormously loud; like sitting on a vibrating stone, that rattles the bones in your ears

- Wings covered in eyes - there is no hiding from the Hearse

name: 6 - Second Transition
text:
**The Walkers are hit by a dust storm; when night sets, they are off-course.**

Description: Quick exposition of events

- Start with regular travel, then dust storm; growling grows louder


- That night, one of the walkers is taken (shriek/scream, belly-rumble grows louder)
- When the dust settles, one of their number is gone

name: 7 - Wilderness
text:
**The Walkers are lost in the trackless waste, and try to find shelter each night.**

The duststorm continues without relenting...

The hearse will kill and eat exactly one member of the caravan each knight you fail to find shelter: “Just room for one inside, sir.”

Describe travel - possible shelters

- Any shelters they find are dangerous too though, usually because they are inhabited

- One idea: list of random possible shelters you may come across between the set shelters known to the walkers, and the dangers within these random shelters

- One place they take shelter - the Hive House (house shaped and structured like a beehive)

- Another Hiding Place: Under the Dry Boardwalk (beside a parched old pond; there is a magic toad underneath which will cure one party member of poison if you agree to carry it to a fresh pond)

  - You can ask X the Walker to use his Velvet Mirror on the shadows under the Boardwalk. If you do, the shadows will reveal themselves to be unquiet ghosts, broken and maddened, from when the lake was full; you may them to rest by pouring water over them, at which time the shadows disappear

- Another shelter-place: The Fossil-Shell (Shell of some giant turtle-titan Blight)

  - Currently inhabited by tribal necromancers who command swarms of lethal, undead moths; use a lamp to distract the moths while you cut down the necromancers

Wrathmaige desperate to get a reading, and finally; they are too far north, and rush south against the setting sun

- Just as they reach shelter, another walker is taken

CCK can't see the shelter in the dark, but see's the silhouette of the bridge

name: 8 - Third Transition
text:
**CCK and his lady and The Walkers of Whiteye come across The Waste Bridge, and prepare to cross.**

The Caravan has to cross "The Waste Bridge" - a bridge in the middle, spanning a gap the size of the grand canyon

- The bridge starts it wide, but gets progressively thinner - at the middle, it splits into two thin tracks - just enough for a single person to walk on either, or for a specially-axled wagon to roll across both - no ruts, just a death-drop on either side

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe
text:
**While crossing the bridge, Wrathmaige is bitten by a poisonous beetle; he cannot keep pace, so the CCK and lady stay with him in the open.**

While they are crossing the bridge, the likeable walker is is bitten by a poisonous beetle:

- Ironic, that the thing which killed him wasn't the hearse

- He tells story to reflect on last moments as one of *The Three Strangers*

- The poison/beetle he is bitten with is the same one that afflicts the lady in *The Harridan’s Moon*

Wrathmaige can't keep up; theo ther walkers march on without him; Wrathmaige teels CCK to go on, Middlemoss is close, he will die anyway; CCK would for Lady, but she refuses

The sun is past noon, the CCK knows they can't find shelter; says if he could see it, in the day, he could kill it with his sword

- Wrathmaige explains the song that will summon the Hearse

The Hearse is finally defeated by playing a counter-melody to its stomach-song; a luring tune that attracts the creature even when it is sated, and forces it to land

name: 10 - Fourth Transition
text: **The CCK, his lady, and Wrathmaige kill the Hearse, watch it breathe its last, then consider the remaining path.**

name: 11 - Future and Finale
text:
**The hero and lover enter a weird, wonderful new future.**

Begin with the **Main Motive** - recomposed, or seen in a new light

The hero and lover awaken to a new understanding of one another

They stare at the wonderful new landscape around them

- **Main Motive** echoed here again, once more seen in a new light

Characters from the other sections gather around

Main character finally achieves acceptance/enlightenment

Culmination of the **Main Motive**; Huge climax

- “They live happily ever after.”

name: The Three Guests
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady listen to stories told by three other travelers in Cobweb's Hotel.**

The stories are told by people whose fates (or stories) mirror possible outcomes for the CCK.

- One story is told by Wrathmaige, who is dying.
- One story is told by R, the Killer.
- One story is told by a woman whose brother/husband was stolen by a doppelganger skeleton.

The stories are still in 2nd-person (that is how they're told in the setting).

The Setup sets the stage - each part is one person's story.

name: Single Act
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady listen to stories told by three other travelers in Cobweb's Hotel.**

The stories are told by people whose fates (or stories) mirror possible outcomes for the CCK.

- One story is told by Wrathmaige, who is dying.
- One story is told by R, the Killer.
- One story is told by a woman whose brother/husband was stolen by a doppelganger skeleton.

The stories are still in 2nd-person (that is how they're told in the setting).

The Setup sets the stage - each part is one person's story.

name: Single Chapter
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady listen to stories told by three other travelers in Cobweb's Hotel.**

The stories are told by people whose fates (or stories) mirror possible outcomes for the CCK.

- One story is told by Wrathmaige, who is dying.
