name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**Part 1: The CCK is leading his Lady down Albino Fish's Way; the lightless ledges deep in Middlemoss Ravine.**

Description: Expository description of Albino Fish's Way - Bold Text.

Action: The CCK is attacked by The Veyers (Conveyors); dozens of restless skeletons without the tops of their skulls

Description: List of maladies the CCK can contract from Veyers (short; 7 items, 1 for each god).

Action: special swordfighting technique needed for Veyers; dealing too much damage at once makes them berzerk; have to whittle

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**The CCK and Lady meet Arras - the ghost who has survived in the tapestry of bark on the dead redwood.**

Dialogue: Arras welcomes them with images grown on its tapestry.

Reflection: The CCK is reminded of another redwood that was important in his childhood.

Dialogue: Arras invites them deeper, into the fragrant hollow.

Description: They walk along the great redwood trunk, with its glowing mushrooms, and its branching paths.

Dialogue: They find dialogue record talking about ghost factory.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**Part 3: The CCK and Lady, finally inside the Factory, meet and speak with a variety of ghosts.**

Action: The CCK has to avoid detection inside The Factory.

Description: List of ghosts the CCK can meet inside the factory.

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**The CCK and Lady continue to search the Factory.**

Dialogue: The Lady wonders about the factory.

Description: List of factory “classes” - classifications of ghosts, and how they are processed.

Reflection: The CCK wonders if he ever knew anyone whose ghost passed through a factory.

Description: They enter the dome/kiln of the Written Stump.

Dialogue: They meet the ghost of Magpie, from A Funeral on the Road of Graves.

Description: (*Extremely Brief*) They free the prostitute's ghost.

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**Part 5: The CCK and Lady find the ghost of Hemlock, but she is maddened by it's factory existence.**

Dialogue-Action: The CCK and Lady find Hemlock, but she is not as they expect.

Dialogue: Hemlock finally recognizes the presence of the CCK and Lady, and wonders sadly to herself.

Dialogue-Description: Exposition describing the purpose of the factory.

Dialogue: Hemlock tries to speak to the CCK again, but cannot hear through the pale purring of the other ghosts.

Dialogue-Reflection: From soul perspective, death is like standing on a thin rusted scaffold over a vast cliff at night.

Dialogue-Action: Hemlock says that the Overseer is coming, she must be one of the Clowder, and hide.

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**Part 6: They have to escape the Factory by way of the Bracken Flue.**

Description: The Overseer Bold Text

Action: Chase Part 1 - The CCK and Lady must find the entrance to The Bracken Flue, the only way out.

Action: Chase Part 2 - Begin climb up the flue.

Action: Chase Part 3 - Last stage of the fight is a difficult choice of 3 bridges.

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**They come out in a hidden glade in Middlemoss Gorge, dominated by a statue, ringed by cliffs.**

Dialogue: The Lady says she thinks they are safe, and is overawed by the scene they find.

Description: Describe the hidden glade.

Reflection: The CCK and his Lady see the daughter, who "speaks" only through the CCK's thoughts.

Action: Describe the way to get out of the Glade.

Reflection: (BRIEF) the CCK question's who's thoughts are the ghost's, who's the knight's?

Description: In a time of dying...

name: The Old Soulwood
text:
**The CCK is lured by a glow wisp into The Old Soulwood; meanwhile, The Blue Lady befriends a a Periwinkle Forest Cat.**

1 - The Blue Lady speaks with a strange Great Forest Cat.

2 - The CCK and Lady are lost in The Soulwood.

3 - The CCK is drawn away by the wisp against his will.

4 - The Lady thanks the cat for leading her to its lair, sheltering her from the storm.

5 - The CCK must bargain with The Tree with the Face of a Lover.

6 - The Lady asks the cat if it loves its home as she loved hers.

7 - The CCK must go through The Underpass.

8 - The lady exposits to the Periwinkle Forest Cat about her growing affection for the CCK.

9 - CCK is confronted by Vimchandler/Lampadephore, The Maker of Wisps.

10 - The Lady notices the rain stops; then the cat growls, as someone approaches the door.

11 - The CCK and Lady are reunited.

name: 1 - Prelude
text:
**1 - The Blue Lady speaks with a strange Periwinkle Forest Cat.**

Dialogue: The Blue Lady nervously greets the cat.

Dialogue-description: She asks the animal if it knows of a place for shelter.

Dialogue-exposition: The Blue Lady tells the animal how she lost her knight.

name: 2 - Opening Conflict
text:
**2 - The CCK and Lady are lost in The Soulwood.**

Action: The CCK finds himself surrounded by trees, with a strangely directionless road.

Reflection: The CCK must admit to himself that he is lost.

Description: Exposition on the history The Old Soulwood.

name: 3 - Mentor
text:
**3 - The CCK is drawn away by a wisp against his will.**

Description: The CCK sees a strange 'light of music', and is drawn toward it.

name: 4 - First Transition
text:
**4 - The Lady thanks the cat for leading her to its lair, sheltering her from the storm.**

Dialogue: Inside the Periwinkle Forest Cat's den, the Blue Lady thanks the cat.

name: 5 - New Lover
text:
**5 - The CCK must bargain with The Tree with the Face of a Lover**.

Reflection: The CCK admits that he has been led on by the light-music.

Description: The CCK finds a strange fenced and gated dry tract.

Action: The CCK must resist the advances of the beautiful tree.

Action: The Tree will let him go and give him a guide.

name: 6 - Second Transition
text:
**6 - The Lady asks the cat if it loves its home as she loved hers.**

Dialogue: The Lady admits to cat that she knows her daughter betrayed their family.

name: 7 - Wilderness
text:
**7 - The CCK must go through The Underpass.**

Description: The CCK enters The Underpass, the root-carved caverns under the road.

Exposition: The Periwinkle Forest Cat - bold text.

Action: The CCK will inevitably draw the attention of the cats, and must fight his way free.

name: 8 - Third Transition
text:
**8 - The lady exposits to the Periwinkle Forest Cat about her growing affection for the CCK.**

Dialogue: The Lady tells the cat that she cannot sleep, she is too worried about her knight.

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe
text:
**9 - CCK is confronted by Lampagogue, The Maker of Wisps.**

Description: The CCK meets Lampagogue.

Action 1: Optional fight with Eyeball Millipede.

Action 2: Optional fight with Glass Wasp.

Action 3: Optional fight with The Padfooted Woodsman.

name: 10 - Fourth Transition
text:
**10 - The cat growls, as someone approaches the door.**

The Lady is telling the cat she should be on her way, when they are interrupted.

name: 11 - Future and Finale
text:
**11 - The CCK and Lady are reunited.**

Action: The CCK kills the Periwinkle Cat.

Dialogue: The Lady laments that the cat had to die, yet is glad to see her knight.

Description: Transition of their passage through the forest, to reach the edge of The Canopy of the Ents.

Dialogue: The Lady asks the knight what he dreams about, knowing he will not answer.

In Time of Dying...

name: The House of Maze
text:
**The CCK and Lady are captured as slaves in The House of Maze, when they seek answers in Middlemoss.**

Part 1: The CCK and Lady are tricked into The House of Maze, and "imprisoned" in the house.

Part 2: The CCK and Lady explore the house maze, and meet the mad prisoners.

Part 3: One former guildman tells them, "Only a thief can navigate the house."

Part 4: The CCK and Lady confront The Master of the Puzzle Mask and The Rat Fiddler.

name: Setup
text:
**Part 1: The CCK and Lady are tricked into The House of Maze, and "imprisoned" in the house.**

Description: group of peasants beating a giant burglar-rat to death with brooms and shovels and sticks.

Exposition: Middlemoss - its history, and layout.

Dialogue: A bystander (a local leech) tells lady that the rats steal things, especially documents

Action: Reluctantly, at his Lady's suggestion that they consult the guildmaster, the CCK leads her inside.

name: Conflict
text:
**Part 2: The CCK and Lady explore the house maze, and meet the mad prisoners.**

Reflection: The CCK remembers another time he was trapped in a Maze House, and what he learned.

Action: Describe the Dust Slimes that guard the house, and how CCK will fight in cramped space.

Description: Give a list of four different "Safe Zones" to fight slimes in.

Dialogue: The Blue Lady compares Middlemoss to The Lake of Tomatoes, hoping to lift the CCK's mood.

Dialogue: The Trapped Cankarnis notices when they enter it's domain in the "Attic".

Action: Fight with The trapped Cankarnis.

Description: The CCK and Lady travel on, beginning to feel the madness of the house, and their hunger.

Dialogue: They meet Oldmust.

name: Drive
text:
**Part 3: The CCK tries to act like a thief (rat).**

Description: Exposition on 'rats in The Time of Dying'.

Reflection: The CCK tries to figure out how a a thief, would think.

Action: Three different ways the CCK can try to become one with the hosue criminals.

Dialogue: Things overheard from the mad and the crooked.

Action: The CCK realizes he can't just think like a rat, he has to act like them.

name: Finale
text:
**Part 4: The CCK and Lady confront The Master of the Puzzle Mask and The Rat Fiddler.**

Description: Bold text: Master's String Parlor.

Action 1: The CCK must learn the patterns of the battle.

Action 2: The CCK, freed from the floor puzzle, must either slay the master or tear off his mask.

Dialogue: If the CCK spares the master's life, he will let them leave, and tell them info.

In the Time of dying...
